will be released 195 (solar) days thereafter, when the
Moon passes through the same constellation according to the laws
of her revolution.
[The commentator discusses whether the days are to be count-
ed according to the civil calendar or the lunar calendar, and comes
to the conclusion that it is the former. In support of this he quotes
I. 16).

The foetuses formed in the bright fortnight will be released in
the dark fortnight and vice versa, those formed in the daytime will
come out at night and vice versa, and those that are formed at dawn
in the evening and vice versa.
